notes: Core technology of midline storage tier; 2-8x capacity advantage over FC/SCSI; ~50% lower cost per GB; max 300 GB per disk (vs 142/72/36 GB for FC/SCSI)
High-performance existing storage tier; high duty-cycle OLTP; used by >50% of enterprises as primary storage; high cost per GB
Legacy high-performance disk interface; high cost per GB; ~50% of enterprises running FC/SCSI; declining relative to SATA for non-OLTP workloads
Enabling reliability technology; ATA disks protected as part of RAID group enabling enterprise-class availability; higher capacity means fewer drives that can fail
Central analytical framework; four principles: Ageing Freezing Accumulation Redundancy; ILM used to justify data placement decisions across storage pyramid
Ultra-performance-intensive database tier; top of storage pyramid; cited as highest-performance tier for specialized workloads
Offline bottom tier of storage pyramid; backup/restore and off-site archiving; slow restore times a key pain point driving midline adoption
ATA-based backup appliance with specialized software; cited as use case for midline; eliminates tape drive/media errors via RAID; 2TB unit $20K-$30K (ATA) vs $35K-$50K (SCSI)
Cited as method to achieve fast data restoration to any point in time; midline storage enables practical CDP implementation
Mixed OLTP/query workload; cited as 'contemporary mixed application' where if data can be segregated mid-line may work for read-heavy portions
